_comment: Master feature test — covers every supported option in the Script runner

speaker._comment: Mode 1a — ref_name without a separate 'name'; ref_name doubles as dialog identity
Mode 1b — ref_name with an explicit 'name' alias
speaker.name: Host

speaker._comment: Mode 3 — voice clone from reference audio in voices/
speaker.name: Donald Trump
speaker.ref_text: Thank you very much. It's a privilege to be here at this forum where leaders in business science art diplomacy and world affairs have gathered for many many years to discuss how we can advance prosperity security and peace.

speaker.name: Character
speaker.ref_description: Female, 34 years old, warm and slightly husky, slow measured pacing, emotionally warm

speaker._comment: Mode 4b — voice design via 'voice' (alias for ref_description)
speaker.name: Wizard
speaker.voice: Male, elderly, deep resonant voice, slow deliberate pacing, wise and mysterious

scenes.title: Scene 1 — Speaker types + scene-level direction + connotation

direction.instruction: quiet indoor conference room, faint air conditioning hum, subtle room tone

direction._comment: Direction with only required fields (defaults used for volume/steps/cfg_scale)
direction.instruction: very soft warm orchestral underscore, barely audible, slow and neutral

dialog.speaker: Narrator
dialog.text: Welcome to the feature test. Every capability of the script engine will be demonstrated.

dialog.speaker: Host
dialog.text: Good morning. Today we test voice cloning, voice design, and built-in voices all in one script.

dialog.speaker: Donald Trump
dialog.text: Tremendous. Absolutely tremendous. This is going to be the greatest audio test you have ever heard.

dialog.speaker: Character
dialog.text: I'm looking forward to hearing what the wizard has to say.

dialog.speaker: Wizard
dialog.text: Patience, young one. All answers reveal themselves in time.

dialog.speaker: Aiden
dialog.text: And I am Aiden — referenced by ref_name directly, without a separate name field.

dialog.speaker: direction
dialog.text: (This line is skipped — speaker 'direction' is always ignored by the renderer)

direction.instruction: outdoor park on a sunny afternoon, light wind, distant children playing

dialog.speaker: Narrator
dialog.text: Scene two tests inline sequential audio effects. Each marker inserts audio at that position and pauses speech around it.

dialog.speaker: Host
dialog.text: A Stable Audio effect with all parameters: [audio:'single loud thunderclap, close and sudden',duration:1.5,pause_before:0.3,pause_after:0.5,volume:0.9,steps:20,cfg_scale:7.0] Did you hear that?

dialog.speaker: Character
dialog.text: And now a file reference from the audio/ folder: [audio:ref_file:'applause',duration:2.0,pause_after:0.4,volume:0.75] Wonderful applause from a pre-recorded file.

dialog.speaker: Host
dialog.text: Multiple effects in one line: [audio:'short door knock, crisp',duration:0.5,pause_after:0.2] [audio:'door creaking open slowly',duration:1.0,pause_after:0.3] Someone is at the door.

dialog.speaker: Wizard
dialog.text: Effect at the start: [audio:'deep magical chime, resonant',duration:1.5,pause_after:0.5] Now I speak after the chime.

dialog.speaker: Donald Trump
dialog.text: Effect at the end, then silence: The crowd loved it. [audio:'crowd cheering and applauding',duration:2.5,pause_before:0.2,volume:0.85]

dialog.speaker: Narrator
dialog.text: Scene three tests background audio — mixed under the speech without interrupting the timeline.

dialog.speaker: Host
dialog.text: [background-audio:'peaceful rain falling on leaves, steady and calming',duration:0,volume:0.3,steps:20,cfg_scale:7.0] Speaking with auto-sized rain in the background. Duration zero means the rain matches my speech length exactly.

dialog.speaker: Character
dialog.text: [background-audio:'soft romantic strings, gentle and warm',duration:5.0,volume:0.25,steps:20] This background has an explicit duration of five seconds, regardless of speech length.

dialog.speaker: Wizard
dialog.text: [background-audio:ref_file:'rain',volume:0.2] Background from a pre-recorded file in audio/ — no Stable Audio generation needed.

dialog.speaker: Donald Trump
dialog.text: [background-audio:'crowd murmur, indoor event, moderate energy',duration:0,volume:0.2,steps:20] Background with pause_before — silence first, then mixing starts: [background-audio:'soft applause fading in',duration:0,pause_before:2.0,volume:0.15,steps:20] The second background starts two seconds into the speech.

dialog.speaker: Host
dialog.text: [background-audio:ref_file:'birds',volume:0.25] [audio:ref_file:'applause',duration:1.5,pause_before:3.0,volume:0.6] Combining a background file with a sequential effect in the same line.

dialog.speaker: Narrator
dialog.text: Final scene tests per-line pause_after overrides.

dialog.speaker: Host
dialog.text: This line has a short pause of 0.3 seconds after it.

dialog.speaker: Character
dialog.text: This line has a long dramatic pause of three seconds after it.

dialog.speaker: Wizard
dialog.text: And this is the final line of the test. No special pause — it uses the global pause_between_lines default.
